refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve and maintain operational availability of ≥0.97 (97%) over the agreed evaluation period, measured as the ratio of time the system is available for productive operation to total scheduled operational time, accounting for planned maintenance, unplanned downtime, and safety-driven shutdowns.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-004 is a key performance indicator (KPI) that directly impacts customer satisfaction and mission success. Refinement clarifies that availability is a measurable, time-based metric with explicit exclusions (planned maintenance) and inclusions (safety-driven events). High safety criticality demands that availability targets do not compromise safety protocols.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Continuous telemetry logging of system state transitions (operational, maintenance, fault, safety-hold); statistical analysis of uptime/downtime over evaluation period; comparison against customer-defined baseline and acceptance threshold.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-004.1: The system shall detect and report faults that impact availability within 5 seconds of occurrence.
FR-NGHE-004.2: The system shall support rapid fault diagnosis and recovery procedures to minimize unplanned downtime.
FR-NGHE-004.3: The system shall maintain redundancy or failover capability for critical subsystems to prevent single-point-of-failure availability loss.
FR-NGHE-004.4: The system shall log all availability-impacting events (faults, maintenance, safety holds) with timestamps for post-evaluation analysis.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Implement continuous health monitoring with automated fault detection, coupled with a modular architecture enabling rapid component replacement and redundant critical subsystems.
DP-B: Deploy a predictive maintenance strategy using machine learning on historical telemetry to forecast failures before they occur, reducing unplanned downtime.

DP-C: Establish a multi-layer availability assurance approach: (1) real-time fault detection and alerting, (2) automated failover for critical functions, (3) remote diagnostics and tele-operation for rapid recovery, (4) comprehensive event logging for availability metrics.
DP-D: Implement a simple scheduled maintenance window strategy with extended operational hours to maximize uptime percentage.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C: Establish a multi-layer availability assurance approach: (1) real-time fault detection and alerting via Safety Services, (2) automated failover for critical Vehicle functions, (3) remote diagnostics and tele-operation capability via Teleoperation module, (4) comprehensive event logging across all modules for availability metrics calculation.

potential_sources: DP-C couples fault detection (Safety Services) with failover logic (Vehicle) and remote recovery (Teleoperation), but these are logically distinct and can be decoupled via well-defined interfaces (SafetyAlert, Telemetry, Tasking).
Event logging spans multiple modules but is aggregated at NGHE Solution level, avoiding tight coupling.
coupling_check.notes: DP-C is preferred over DP-B (predictive maintenance alone is insufficient for high-safety-criticality systems) and DP-D (scheduled maintenance does not guarantee 0.97 availability). DP-A is subsumed within DP-C. Multi-layer approach aligns with safety-critical system design principles.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Fault detection latency (5 sec) is acceptable for availability calculation; risk if safety-critical faults require sub-second response.
Assumption: Teleoperation network link is reliable enough to support remote recovery; risk of network outages reducing availability.
Assumption: Failover logic is deterministic and does not introduce new failure modes; risk of failover bugs reducing availability.
Risk: Availability target (0.97) may be unachievable if Vehicle subsystem MTBF is too low or MTTR is too high; requires validation against component reliability data.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-C to explicitly specify: (a) fault detection threshold and latency SLA, (b) failover decision logic and safety constraints, (c) network redundancy requirements for Teleoperation, (d) event logging schema and availability metric calculation algorithm. Consider adding a sub-DP for predictive maintenance as a secondary availability lever.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: System-level availability orchestration module responsible for aggregating fault signals from Vehicle and Safety Services, coordinating failover decisions, enabling Teleoperation-based remote recovery, and maintaining comprehensive event logs for availability metrics. Acts as the integration point for all availability-assurance mechanisms.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Receives fault and safety-hold signals from Safety Services; triggers availability-impacting event logging and failover evaluation.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives continuous health and state data from Vehicle; used for fault detection confirmation and availability status calculation.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends failover commands and recovery directives to Vehicle; may also coordinate with Teleoperation for remote intervention.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports communication with Teleoperation module for remote diagnostics and recovery; availability of Network Link directly impacts availability KPI.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural system-level owner of availability orchestration. Fault detection logic, failover coordination, and event logging are core system responsibilities. Minimal diffusion: only interfaces to Vehicle, Safety Services, and Teleoperation are external.

Vehicle owns its own fault detection and failover for subsystems, but system-level availability orchestration and cross-module failover coordination belong at NGHE Solution level. High diffusion risk if availability logic is scattered across Vehicle and other modules.

Safety Services owns fault detection and safety-hold logic, but availability metric calculation and failover orchestration are system-level concerns. Assigning availability KPI to Safety Services would conflate safety and availability, violating separation of concerns.
Teleoperation is a recovery mechanism, not an availability orchestrator. Assigning availability KPI to Teleoperation would create circular dependency and high diffusion of availability logic.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the appropriate owner because: (1) it is the system-level integration point, (2) availability is a system-level KPI that requires cross-module coordination, (3) it can aggregate signals from Vehicle (Telemetry), Safety Services (SafetyAlert), and Teleoperation (Network Link status) without tight coupling, (4) it centralizes event logging and metrics calculation, avoiding diffusion.
containment_risks: Risk: If NGHE Solution becomes a monolithic orchestrator, it may become a bottleneck or single point of failure. Mitigation: design NGHE Solution as a thin orchestration layer with clear, decoupled interfaces.

Risk: Network Link availability directly impacts Teleoperation-based recovery; if Network Link is unavailable, remote recovery is impossible, reducing availability. Mitigation: ensure Vehicle has autonomous failover capability independent of Teleoperation.
Risk: Event logging at NGHE Solution level may create performance overhead if Telemetry data rate is high. Mitigation: implement asynchronous, buffered logging with configurable sampling rates.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Clarify the internal architecture of NGHE Solution: (a) define a thin orchestration layer that coordinates fault detection, failover, and logging without implementing domain-specific logic, (b) specify the event logging schema and availability metric calculation algorithm, (c) define the failover decision matrix (which faults trigger which failover actions), (d) ensure Vehicle and Safety Services can operate autonomously if NGHE Solution orchestration layer fails.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-C (multi-layer availability assurance) directly addresses FR-NGHE-004 (achieve ≥0.97 availability). The DP specifies four concrete mechanisms (fault detection, failover, remote recovery, event logging) that collectively enable availability measurement and improvement. No independence violations detected; each mechanism addresses a distinct aspect of availability.

diffusion_risks: Moderate risk: Failover logic may be distributed across Vehicle (local failover) and NGHE Solution (system-level failover coordination). Mitigation: clearly define the boundary between local and system-level failover decisions.

Moderate risk: Event logging spans multiple modules (Vehicle, Safety Services, Teleoperation); NGHE Solution must aggregate without tight coupling. Mitigation: use standardized event schema and asynchronous logging interfaces.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary for availability orchestration. Vehicle and Safety Services remain owners of their respective fault detection and failover logic; NGHE Solution coordinates at the system level. Teleoperation is a recovery mechanism, not an availability owner. Containment score of 0.95 for NGHE Solution is justified.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Availability KPI is measurable via event logs and telemetry. Verification approach: (1) instrument all modules to log availability-impacting events with timestamps, (2) aggregate logs at NGHE Solution level, (3) calculate uptime/downtime ratio over evaluation period, (4) compare against 0.97 threshold. Feasibility is high if event logging is implemented correctly.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: FR-NGHE-004 is allocated to NGHE Solution as the system-level availability orchestrator. Sub-FRs (fault detection, failover, remote recovery, event logging) are allocated to Vehicle, Safety Services, Teleoperation, and NGHE Solution respectively. Allocation is clear and traceable.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP-C to specify failover decision logic and safety constraints explicitly.
Define the event logging schema and availability metric calculation algorithm at NGHE Solution level.
Clarify the boundary between local failover (Vehicle) and system-level failover coordination (NGHE Solution).
Specify Network Link redundancy or failover requirements to ensure Teleoperation-based recovery is reliable.
Add a sub-DP for predictive maintenance as a secondary availability lever, if feasible.

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-004 is a system-level KPI that requires decomposition into sub-FRs addressing fault detection, failover, remote recovery, and event logging. The refined FR and selected DP are sound, and NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary. However, the sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-004.1 through FR-NGHE-004.4) require further decomposition to define concrete design parameters and implementation modules. Proceeding to the next level will clarify the detailed architecture and allocation of availability mechanisms.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-004.1: The system shall detect and report faults that impact availability within 5 seconds of occurrence.
FR-NGHE-004.2: The system shall support rapid fault diagnosis and recovery procedures to minimize unplanned downtime.
FR-NGHE-004.3: The system shall maintain redundancy or failover capability for critical subsystems to prevent single-point-of-failure availability loss.
FR-NGHE-004.4: The system shall log all availability-impacting events (faults, maintenance, safety holds) with timestamps for post-evaluation analysis.

assumptions: Fault detection latency of 5 seconds is acceptable for availability calculation.
Teleoperation network link is reliable enough to support remote recovery.
Failover logic is deterministic and does not introduce new failure modes.
Vehicle subsystem MTBF and MTTR are compatible with 0.97 availability target.
Event logging overhead is acceptable and does not degrade system performance.
Safety-driven shutdowns are excluded from availability downtime calculation.
open_risks: Network Link availability directly impacts Teleoperation-based recovery; if unavailable, remote recovery is impossible.
Failover logic may introduce new failure modes if not carefully designed.
Availability target (0.97) may be unachievable if component reliability is insufficient.
Event logging at NGHE Solution level may create performance bottleneck if Telemetry data rate is high.
Distributed failover logic (Vehicle + NGHE Solution) may create coordination issues or race conditions.
evidence_needed: Component MTBF and MTTR data to validate 0.97 availability achievability.
Network Link reliability specifications and redundancy options.
Failover decision logic and safety constraint specifications.
Event logging schema and availability metric calculation algorithm.
Performance analysis of event logging overhead.
Detailed architecture of NGHE Solution orchestration layer.
